content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>switch to cloud</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-03-26</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

Now I'm seeing actual tool execution. The primary session read the Claude settings.json file, which shows:
- ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set to http://localhost:4000
- ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is set to "sentinel-local"

This is in the context of "switch to cloud" - so it appears the user wants to switch from using a local proxy (localhost:4000) to using the actual Anthropic cloud API.

However, I'm only seeing the READ operation so far - no actual changes have been made yet. According to my instructions, I should skip routine operations like "Simple file listings" and wait for actual deliverables.

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I have actual work being done. The primary session edited the settings.json file to:
- Remove the ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL environment variable (was pointing to http://localhost:4000)
- Remove the ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable (was set to "sentinel-local")
- Set env to an empty object {}

This is in response to the user request "switch to cloud", which means switching from the local LiteLLM/Ollama proxy setup to using the actual Anthropic cloud API.

This is a configuration change. Let me create an observation:

Type: change (this is a configuration modification)
Title: Switched Claude Code from local proxy to Anthropic cloud API
Subtitle: Removed local API endpoint configuration to enable cloud-based API calls
Facts:
- settings.json env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL removed (was http://localhost:4000)
- settings.json env.ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN removed (was "sentinel-local")
- Claude Code now uses default Anthropic cloud endpoints
Files modified: /mnt/c/Users/nugad/.claude/settings.json
Narrative: The Claude Code configuration was updated to switch from a local proxy setup to the Anthropic cloud API. Previously, the environment variables ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed to http://localhost:4000 (a local LiteLLM proxy) and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN was set to "sentinel-local". These settings were removed by emptying the env object in settings.json, allowing Claude Code to use its default behavior of connecting directly to Anthropic's cloud API endpoints with standard authentication.

Concepts:
- what-changed: configuration modification
- how-it-works: API endpoint routing
- trade-off: cloud vs local proxy

Let me refine this observation.

Done. VS Code's Claude Code extension will now talk directly to Anthropic's API using your existing OAuth login (the "OAuth tokens found in secure storage" we saw in the logs earlier).

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Switched Claude Code from local proxy to Anthropic cloud API</title>
  <subtitle>Removed local API endpoint configuration to enable direct cloud-based API calls</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>settings.json env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL removed (was http://localhost:4000)</fact>
    <fact>settings.json env.ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN removed (was "sentinel-local")</fact>
    <fact>Claude Code now uses default Anthropic cloud endpoints instead of local proxy</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Claude Code configuration switched from local proxy to Anthropic cloud API. Previously, environment variables routed all API calls through a local LiteLLM proxy at http://localhost:4000 with a sentinel auth token. These settings were removed by emptying the env object in settings.json, restoring default behavior where Claude Code connects directly to Anthropic's cloud API endpoints using standard authentication. This change affects all subsequent API requests made by Claude Code.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>/mnt/c/Users/nugad/.claude/settings.json</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>/mnt/c/Users/nugad/.claude/settings.json</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
Now there's another configuration change - the Claude model was switched from "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" to "claude-sonnet-4-6" in the VS Code settings.

This is related to the "switch to cloud" request. The user had been using a local proxy with model aliases (based on the memory showing "Claude model aliases"), and now they're switching to the actual cloud model names.

Haiku is a smaller/faster model, and Sonnet is a more capable mid-tier model. This is upgrading from Haiku to Sonnet, and also switching from what appears to be a custom/aliased model identifier to the official cloud model identifier.

Type: change (configuration modification)
Title: Upgraded Claude Code model from Haiku to Sonnet
Subtitle: Changed selected model to claude-sonnet-4-6 for enhanced capabilities
Facts:
- VSCode settings claudeCode.selectedModel changed from "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" to "claude-sonnet-4-6"
- Model upgrade from Haiku (fast/economical) to Sonnet (more capable)
- Using official Anthropic cloud model identifier

Narrative: The Claude Code model selection was upgraded from claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to claude-sonnet-4-6 in VS Code user settings. This change accompanies the switch from local proxy to cloud API, moving from a custom model identifier to the official Anthropic cloud model name. Sonnet provides enhanced reasoning and coding capabilities compared to Haiku, trading some speed for improved output quality.

Concepts:
- what-changed
- trade-off (speed vs capability)
- how-it-works (model selection)

Files modified: /mnt/c/Users/nugad/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/settings.json

This change complements the switch from local proxy to cloud API, transitioning from a custom model identifier to the official Anthropic cloud model name. Sonnet provides significantly enhanced reasoning, coding, and problem-solving capabilities compared to Haiku, with a trade-off of slightly higher latency and cost for improved output quality and accuracy.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is asking for a progress summary of what happened in the primary Claude session. Let me review what I observed:

1. User requested "switch to cloud"
2. Read the Claude settings.json file which had:
   - ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: http://localhost:4000
   - ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN: sentinel-local
3. Edited settings.json to remove those environment variables (emptied the env object)
4. Edited VS Code settings to change the model from "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" to "claude-sonnet-4-6"
5. Claude's response indicated that VS Code will now talk directly to Anthropic's API using OAuth login, and instructed the user to reload the VS Code window

So the work completed was:
- Switched from local LiteLLM/Ollama proxy to Anthropic cloud API
- Upgraded model from Haiku to Sonnet
- Configuration changes made to settings files

This appears to be complete - the user was told to reload VS Code to pick up the changes.

Let me write a summary from the observer perspective, not referencing myself.

content.text:
```xml
<summary>
  <request>Switch Claude Code from local proxy to Anthropic cloud API</request>
  <investigated>Claude settings.json configuration showing local proxy setup with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointing to localhost:4000 and custom auth token. VS Code user settings showing model selection of claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.</investigated>
  <learned>Claude Code was configured to route API requests through a local LiteLLM proxy at localhost:4000 using custom environment variables. Removing these env vars restores default behavior of connecting directly to Anthropic's cloud API using OAuth authentication already stored in VS Code's secure storage. The local proxy stack remains operational but unused by VS Code after this change.</learned>
  <completed>Removed ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from Claude settings.json env configuration. Upgraded selected model from claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to claude-sonnet-4-6 in VS Code user settings. Claude Code now configured to connect directly to Anthropic cloud API with enhanced Sonnet model.</completed>
  <next_steps>User needs to reload VS Code window to apply the configuration changes. The session appears complete pending the window reload.</next_steps>
  <notes>The switch preserves the existing local LiteLLM/Ollama infrastructure while redirecting only VS Code's Claude Code extension to use cloud services. Model upgrade from Haiku to Sonnet provides better capabilities at the cost of slightly higher latency and API usage costs.</notes>
</summary>
```
